revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 画像=| 名称=貴船神社| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市古川町貴船町8-6| 祭神=*[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]など| 社格=式内社（論社...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 画像=| 名称=貴船神社| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市古川町貴船町8-6| 祭神=*[[淤加美神|高靇大神]]など| 社格=式内社（論社）| 例祭=}}
'''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]（旧[[吉城郡]][[古川町 (岐阜県)|古川町]]）にある[[神社]]。
[[飛騨国]][[吉城郡|荒城郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式名社]]の高田神社の論社である（別の論社は[[高田神社 (飛騨市)|高田神社]]）。
== 概略 == 創建時期は不明。[[室町時代]]以降式内社の高田神社は衰退し、所在が不明となったという。
[[江戸時代]]になり式内社高田神社は、古川郷是重の貴船神社か古川郷太江の白山社かが論争になっていた。式内社の高田神社は「あがたじんじゃ」が正式で、「たかだじんじゃ」と誤って呼称されたものである。貴船神社付近の地名「字高田」が特別な神域であったことが、貴船神社が式内社高田神社である根拠とされていた。
この論争は、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により、太江に鎮座する白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、白山社は高田神社に改称することとなる。しかし、貴船神社が式内社高田神社という説は強く、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）の[[高山県]]の飛騨國式内式外社取調書上には高田神社を所在未詳とされていた。正式に白山社が式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。
貴船神社が高田神社に改称されることはなかったが、現在も式内社高田神社の論社である。
== 祭神 == *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] *[[大山咋神|大山咋大神]] *高照光姫命 *[[年神|大年神]]
== 交通機関 == *[[高山本線]][[飛騨古川駅]]より約1km
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやひたし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:飛騨市]]

貴船神社が高田神社に改称されることはなかったが、現在も式内社高田神社の論社である。

== 祭神 == * [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] * [[大山咋神|大山咋大神]] * 高照光姫命 * [[大年神]]
== 由緒 == 創建時期は不明。[[室町時代]]以降式内社の高田神社は衰退し、所在が不明となったという。
[[江戸時代]]になり式内社高田神社は、古川郷是重の貴船神社か古川郷太江の白山社かが論争になっていた。式内社の高田神社は「あがたじんじゃ」が正式で、「たかだじんじゃ」と誤って呼称されたものである。貴船神社付近の地名「字高田」が特別な神域であったことが、貴船神社が式内社高田神社である根拠とされていた。
この論争は、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により、太江に鎮座する白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、白山社は高田神社に改称することとなる。しかし、貴船神社が式内社高田神社という説は強く、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）の[[高山県]]の飛騨國式内式外社取調書上には高田神社を所在未詳とされていた。正式に白山社が式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。
貴船神社が高田神社に改称されることはなかったが、現在も式内社高田神社の論社である。
== 交通機関 == * [[高山本線]][[飛騨古川駅]]より約1km
== 関連項目 == * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]

{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像=[[File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]にある[[神社]]。
[[飛騨国]][[荒城郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式名社]]の高田神社の[[論社]]である（別の論社は[[高田神社 (飛騨市)|高田神社]]）。
== 祭神 == * [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] * [[大山咋神|大山咋大神]] * 高照光姫命 * [[大年神]]
== 由緒 == 創建時期は不明。[[室町時代]]以降式内社の高田神社は衰退し、所在が不明となったという。
[[江戸時代]]になり式内社高田神社は、古川郷是重の貴船神社か古川郷太江の白山社かが論争になっていた。式内社の高田神社は「あがたじんじゃ」が正式で、「たかだじんじゃ」と誤って呼称されたものである。貴船神社付近の地名「字高田」が特別な神域であったことが、貴船神社が式内社高田神社である根拠とされていた。
この論争は、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により、太江に鎮座する白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、白山社は高田神社に改称することとなる。しかし、貴船神社が式内社高田神社という説は強く、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）の[[高山県]]の飛騨國式内式外社取調書上には高田神社を所在未詳とされていた。正式に白山社が式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。
貴船神社が高田神社に改称されることはなかったが、現在も式内社高田神社の論社である。
== 交通機関 == * [[高山本線]][[飛騨古川駅]]より約1km
== 関連項目 == * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|ja|飛騨古川駅}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|ja|飛騨古川駅}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg|250px]] |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|ja|飛騨古川駅}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg|250px]] |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|ja|飛騨古川駅}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg|250px]] |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|ja|飛騨古川駅}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|ja|飛騨古川駅}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2nd year }} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3年}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|ja|飛騨古川駅}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}.
== Related Articles ==

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg|250px]] |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|ja|飛騨市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Oka-mi-no-kami|ja|淤加美神}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2nd year }} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|Meiji 3|ja|明治3rd year }} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|ja|飛騨古川駅}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|qq=|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|qq=|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|qq=|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|qq=|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].
== Related Articles ==

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].
== Related Articles ==

* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.

== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. == Enshrined Deities ==

== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
