* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (飛騨市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (飛騨市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634813]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from [[:en:Hida-Furukawa Station|Hida-Furukawa Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].

It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hida, Gifu|Hida, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. == Enshrined Deities ==
* [[:en:Kuraokami|Oka-mi-no-kami]] (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==

The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until [[:en:1870|Meiji 3]] (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==

It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==

{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.

{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319|1=Hida, Gifu}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|1=Kuraokami}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida, Gifu|1=Hida, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki District, Gifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami|1=Kuraokami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3|1=1870}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station|1=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Hida Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665887}}

The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3|1=1870}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.

{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319|1=Hida, Gifu}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|1=Kuraokami}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida, Gifu|1=Hida, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki District, Gifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami|1=Kuraokami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3|1=1870}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station|1=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}.

The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3|1=1870}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station|1=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}.

{{infobox religious building |name = Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)|native_name = 貴船神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Kifune shrine, Hida, 2015.jpg |address = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]古川町貴船町8-6 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|13|44.2|N|137|11|25.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高龗大神]] |社格 = 式内社（論社） |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319|1=Hida, Gifu}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.228944|N|137.190417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|1=Kuraokami}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hida, Gifu|en|Hida, Gifu|WD=Q847319|lt=Hida, Gifu|1=Hida, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered a possible location (論社) for {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|ja|高田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida Province)|1=Takata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124481862}}, an {{ill|Enshrinement in the Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement in the Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}-listed shrine of {{ill|Araki District, Hida Province|ja|荒城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki District, Hida Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉城郡|1=Yoshiki District, Gifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8056123}}, Hida Province. Another contender for this title is {{ill|Takada Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Shrine (Hida)|1=Takata Shrine (Hida)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672195}}.
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Oka-mi-no-kami|1=Kuraokami}} (Takao-no-kami, 高龗大神) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山咋大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takateruhikarihime-no-mikoto (高照光姫命) * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}
== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3|1=1870}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|WD=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station|1=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}.

== History ==
The exact date of Kifune Shrine’s founding is unknown. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} onward, the shrine known as Takada Shrine declined, and its precise location was lost.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, a debate arose over whether the historic Takada Shrine was actually Kifune Shrine in Koreju, Furukawa-go, or the Hakusan Shrine in Oe, Furukawa-go. The correct reading of the name 高田神社 should be "Agata Shrine" (あがたじんじゃ), but it was mistakenly read as "Takada Shrine" (たかだじんじゃ). Supporters of Kifune Shrine pointed to the local place name "Takada" (字高田) as evidence that it had been a sacred site, arguing that this indicated Kifune Shrine was the true Takada Shrine.
This debate was settled in the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} when the {{ill|Yoshida family (Shinto)|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family (Shinto)|qq=}}, a prominent Shinto priestly family, identified Hakusan Shrine in Oe as the true site of Takada Shrine. The shrine was accordingly renamed Takada Shrine. However, Kifune Shrine's claim remained strong, and in {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), an investigation by Takayama Prefecture found the location of Takada Shrine to be "unverified." It was not until {{ill|1870|en|1870|qid=Q7741|lt=Meiji 3|1=1870}} (1870) that the Hakusan Shrine was officially recognized as the true Takada Shrine.
Although Kifune Shrine was not renamed Takada Shrine, it remains recognized as a possible location (論社) for the historical Takada Shrine.
== Transportation ==
* Approximately 1 km from {{ill|Hida-Furukawa Station|en|Hida-Furukawa Station|qid=Q875450|lt=Hida-Furukawa Station|1=Hida-Furukawa Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|qid=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}.
